key_insight: The paper identifies a single "cliff token" that sharply drops token-wise potential and acts as the trigger point where mathematical reasoning traces shift toward failure.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical way to diagnose, intervene in, and train LLMs for more robust math reasoning by targeting one token rather than entire failed chains.
method: Detect cliff tokens with an adaptive threshold on token-wise potential using a one-sided two-proportion z-test.
Classify cliffs into deterministic, uncertain, and sampled-off types using greedy choice and token entropy.
Apply single-token preference optimization at cliff positions (Cliff-DPO) to improve reasoning behavior.
evidence_signal: Across seven models and three benchmarks (GSM1K, MATH500, AIME 2025), deleting the first cliff token and resampling recovered pass@64 to 1.0, and Cliff-DPO improved accuracy by up to +6.6.
watch_item: Cliff detection and Cliff-DPO may depend on benchmark/model-specific potential and entropy calibration, so transferability to other tasks or decoding settings should be validated carefully.
paper_title: Cliff Tokens: Identifying Single-Token Failure Triggers in LLM Mathematical Reasoning
